title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry lips
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry lips are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, created by soaking plant material in alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips by delivering beneficial compounds directly to the affected area. Some examples of herbal tinctures for dry lips include calendula, chamomile, and lavender. These natural remedies have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties, which can alleviate discomfort, reduce redness and swelling, and promote tissue repair. By incorporating herbal tinctures into a lip care routine, individuals can experience relief from dryness, leading to improved comfort and confidence in their daily lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture helps with dry lips because it contains numerous bioactive compounds, including vitamins, minerals, amino acids, and antioxidants. These components work together to soothe, moisturize, and protect chapped lips. The gel-like substance in aloe vera forms a protective barrier on the lips, preventing further dryness and irritation. Additionally, aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling caused by severe dryness or cracked lips. Its humectant nature allows it to draw and lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and supple. Regular application of aloe vera tincture on dry lips can provide long-lasting relief and promote overall lip health.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Aloe vera tincture contains polysaccharides that form a protective layer on the lips, locking in moisture and preventing dryness.
Healing: Aloe vera tincture has anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties due to the presence of compounds like acemannan, which promotes tissue regeneration and reduces redness and irritation.
Soothing: Aloe vera tincture contains glycoproteins and anthraquinones that have analgesic and anti-pruritic effects, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with dry lips.

Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tincture has potent anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of salicylic acid, bradykinase, and other compounds, which help reduce swelling and redness caused by dry lips.

Antimicrobial: Aloe vera tincture has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of compounds like lupeol, phenols, and sulfur, which help prevent infection and promote wound healing in dry, cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant are the most commonly used part for making medicinal tinctures for dry lips. They contain a gel-like substance that is rich in vitamins, minerals, and enzymes which can help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.

Stems: While not as commonly used as the leaves, the stems of the aloe vera plant can also be used to make medicinal tinctures for dry lips. They contain a sap that has similar properties to the gel in the leaves, and can help to hydrate and soothe dry lips.

Seeds: The seeds of the aloe vera plant are not typically used to make medicinal tinctures for dry lips, as they do not contain the same moisturizing and soothing properties as the leaves and stems. However, they can be used to extract oil, which is often used in cosmetics for its emollient properties.

Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant are also not typically used to make medicinal tinctures for dry lips. While they do contain some beneficial compounds, they are not as rich in the moisturizing and soothing properties as the leaves and stems.

Roots: The roots of the aloe vera plant are not typically used to make medicinal tinctures for dry lips. They contain different compounds than the leaves and stems, and are typically used for other medicinal purposes.
Rhizomes: Aloe vera does not have Rhizomes.
Bark: Aloe vera does not have bark.
Buds: Aloe vera does not produce buds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Wash the aloe vera leaves thoroughly with cold water and pat dry with a clean towel.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in 100 proof vodka for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, reserving the liquid as your tincture.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with dry lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate chapped lips. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, has been used for centuries in skincare due to its ability to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration. When applied to dry lips, the tincture creates a barrier that locks in moisture, preventing further dryness and protecting the lips from environmental stressors. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial and antiviral properties that can help prevent infections and speed up the healing process of cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Pot marigold tincture contains mucilage, a substance that helps to lock in moisture, providing relief to dry and chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, thanks to its flavonoid content, which helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on dry lips.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties due to its active constituents like lutein and quercetin, aiding in the prevention of lip infections and promoting faster healing.
Antioxidant: The tincture contains antioxidants like carotenoids and flavonoids, which protect the lips from free radical damage, reducing the signs of aging and promoting healthy lip tissue.
Soothing: Pot marigold tincture has a soothing effect on dry lips, thanks to its saponin content, providing relief from discomfort and irritation caused by dryness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, pot marigold leaves can provide nutrients and assist in healing dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, pot marigold seeds can help nourish and protect the lips, promoting softness and hydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in quantities sufficient for preparation.
Clean and chop the harvested flowers to release their essence and prepare for extraction.
Combine chopped flowers with 20% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and let sit for 4-6 weeks.
Shake the mixture daily for the first week and then weekly for the remaining period to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard solids after completion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tincture is extracted from the flowers of the German chamomile plant, which contains azulene, a compound known for its soothing effects on irritated skin. When applied to dry lips, the tincture can help reduce inflammation and redness, while also providing much-needed moisture. Its antibacterial properties can also help prevent infection and promote healing of chapped or cracked lips. Additionally, German chamomile tincture contains essential oils and antioxidants that can help protect the lips from environmental stressors, keeping them soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief to dry, cracked lips. The active constituent responsible for this property is the compound chamazulene.

Moisturizing: Chamomile tincture has hydrating properties that help keep lips soft and supple, preventing dryness and chapping. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils in chamomile.

Wound healing: German chamomile tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, aiding in the recovery of damaged lips. This is attributed to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the flavonoids and terpenoids found in chamomile.

Pain relief: Chamomile tincture has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips. The active constituent responsible for this is the bisabolol oxide A.

Antimicrobial: German chamomile tincture has antimicrobial properties that help protect lips from infection and promote overall lip health. This is due to the presence of various active constituents, including azulene, bisabolol, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, chamomile flowers can help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile leaves can help protect and heal dry, irritated lips.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, chamomile seeds can provide intense hydration and nourishment for dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak potency during full moon.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently rinsing with filtered water and air drying overnight.
Combine 200g of cleaned chamomile flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking jar daily during maceration period.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard solids after 2 hours of steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with dry lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate chapped lips. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, which can help to repair damaged lip tissue and alleviate dryness. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture can help to stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that is essential for maintaining healthy skin and preventing dryness. By applying St. John's wort tincture to dry lips, you can provide immediate relief and long-term protection against dryness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate dry, cracked lips caused by irritation or environmental factors. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.
Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, which can help repair damaged lip tissue and restore moisture. This is due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids in St. John's wort.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help soothe dry, sore lips. Hypericin and hyperforin are the primary active constituents responsible for this effect.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can help prevent lip infections and promote overall lip health. Hyperforin is the primary active constituent responsible for St. John's wort's antimicrobial properties.
Moisturizing: St. John's wort tincture has moisturizing properties that can help hydrate and protect dry lips. This is attributed to the presence of essential oils and waxes in the plant.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and hypericin, St. John's Wort flowers help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: High in tannins, St. John's Wort leaves provide astringent properties, aiding in the restoration of dry lip moisture balance.
Buds: Packed with antioxidants, St. John's Wort buds help protect and nourish dry, damaged lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest st john's wort flowers and leaves from a trusted source in midsummer at peak potency.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove dirt and debris immediately.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar tightly sealable.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every few days regularly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with dry lips because it has moisturizing and soothing properties that can alleviate dryness and discomfort. The tincture is often made by steeping lavender flowers in a mixture of water and alcohol, which extracts the beneficial compounds found in the plant. These compounds include tannins, essential oils, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied to the lips, the tincture can help to reduce inflammation, soothe dryness, and promote healing. Additionally, lavender has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infection and keep the lips healthy. Overall, the moisturizing, soothing, and protective effects of English lavender tincture make it a useful remedy for dry lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing property: English lavender tincture contains emollient compounds that help soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips, preventing further cracking and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, tannins, and polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by severely dry or cracked lips. This is attributed to the presence of linalyl acetate, linalool, and other terpenes in lavender.

Wound-healing property: English lavender tincture promotes tissue regeneration, which can help accelerate the healing process of dry, chapped lips. This is facilitated by the active constituents, such as eucalyptol, camphor, and flavonoids, that stimulate collagen production.

Analgesic property: Lavender tincture has natural pain-relieving properties that can help reduce the discomfort and pain associated with dry, cracked lips. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and caryophyllene.

Antimicrobial property: The tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties that help protect dry lips from infection and promote overall lip health. This is due to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol, camphor, and tannins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, lavender flowers help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, lavender leaves can help reduce redness and irritation on dry lips.
Buds: High in essential oils, lavender buds provide nourishment and hydration to dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in large quantities for optimal yield.
Weigh out 200-300 grams of the harvested flowers and place them into a clean glass jar.
Cover the jar with a lid and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow infusing.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding solids.
Add 50-60% vodka by volume to the liquid extract, shaking well and storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can moisturize and heal chapped lips. The tincture is made from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, which contains compounds that can help relieve irritation and reduce redness. When applied to dry lips, it creates a protective barrier that locks in moisture, preventing further dryness and promoting healthy lip tissue. Additionally, lemon balm has antiviral properties that can help prevent cold sores, a common cause of dry, cracked lips. Overall, lemon balm tincture is a natural and effective remedy for dry, uncomfortable lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Lemon balm tincture contains compounds that help to soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips, providing immediate relief and long-term nourishment. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols and flavonoids, which have emollient and humectant properties.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, making it an effective treatment for dry, cracked lips. The rosmarinic acid and eugenol in lemon balm are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antiviral: Lemon balm tincture has antiviral properties that help protect the lips from infections and speed up healing. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is citral, which has been shown to inhibit the herpes simplex virus.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm tincture contains powerful antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as pollution and UV radiation, which can cause dryness and aging. The rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid in lemon balm are responsible for its antioxidant properties.

Soothing: Lemon balm tincture has a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, and providing relief from dryness and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, which have astringent properties, and terpenes, which have a soothing effect on the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for dry lips due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils that can help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating dry, irritated lips.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain some beneficial compounds that can help soothe and hydrate dry lips.

Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for dry lips, as they can help to nourish and protect the delicate skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves or purchase dried leaf material in sufficient quantity for your needs weighing approximately 20-30 grams.
Clean and sort the herb material removing any debris or damaged leaves to ensure optimal quality.
Fill a clean glass jar with the prepared herb material leaving about an inch at the top for expansion during soaking.
Add high-proof vodka or everclear grain alcohol in a ratio of 1:3 (herb to liquid) allowing it to infuse for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with dry lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate chapped lips. The herb's mucilage content forms a protective barrier over the lips, locking in moisture and shielding them from environmental stressors. Additionally, plantain tincture has been found to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help repair damaged lip tissue and alleviate dryness. Its soothing and calming effects can provide instant relief to dry, cracked, and inflamed lips, leaving them feeling soft, supple, and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Plantain tincture contains mucilaginous compounds that form a protective film, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry, cracked lips (active constituents
Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture contains antimicrobial compounds, which help prevent infections and promote wound healing in dry, damaged lip tissue (active constituents
Antioxidant: Plantain tincture contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect lips from environmental stressors and promote skin cell regeneration (active constituents
Soothing: Plantain tincture has a soothing effect on dry lips due to its demulcent properties, helping to alleviate discomfort and promoting overall lip health (active constituents
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in iridoid glycosides and other compounds, plantain leaves are used in tinctures for their moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief for dry and chapped lips.
Seeds: Plantain seeds contain high levels of mucilage, which helps to soothe, protect, and hydrate dry lips when used in medicinal tinctures.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant has astringent properties due to its tannin content, making it ideal for tinctures that help to tighten and protect dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves from a clean and healthy environment.
Dry the collected plantago major leaves using air circulation method.
Chop the dried plantago major leaves into small pieces and macerate them.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper.
Filter the tincture extract through a fine-mesh strainer or filter paper.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrowleaf plantain tincture helps with dry lips because it contains mucilaginous compounds that form a protective layer over the lips, helping to lock in moisture and soothe irritation. Rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, it can help reduce redness and inflammation caused by chapping, while also promoting faster healing of damaged skin. Furthermore, the tincture's emollient properties help to soften and smooth the lips, leaving them feeling hydrated and supple. Regular application of narrowleaf plantain tincture can provide long-lasting relief from dry, cracked lips, and help maintain optimal lip health.
remedy_properties: Wound healing: Narrowleaf plantain tincture contains mucilage and tannins that help soothe and heal chapped, dry lips by forming a protective layer and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and iridoid glycosides found in plantain tincture help alleviate dry lips by reducing inflammation and swelling, providing relief from irritation.

Antimicrobial: Narrowleaf plantain tincture's antibacterial and antiviral properties, due to its allantoin and aucubin content, help prevent infections and promote faster healing of dry, cracked lips.
Hydration: The mucilage in plantain tincture attracts and retains moisture, keeping lips hydrated and supple, while allantoin stimulates cellular regeneration and softens the skin.
Pain relief: Containing analgesic compounds like aucubin, plantain tincture helps alleviate pain and discomfort caused by dry, chapped lips, providing instant relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in mucilage, the leaves help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing high levels of oil, the seeds can be used to create a nourishing lip balm.
Flowers: With anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, the flowers can help protect and heal dry, irritated lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh narrowleaf plantain leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 2 weeks.
Chop dried plantain leaves into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to macerate for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Filter the tincture one more time and transfer to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cherry tincture helps with dry lips because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal chapped lips. The tincture is made from the bark and leaves of the black cherry tree, which are rich in vitamins and minerals that can help moisturize and protect the lips from environmental stressors. Additionally, black cherry tincture has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including dryness and irritation, due to its astringent and cooling effects. By applying the tincture directly to the lips, it can provide instant relief and long-term hydration, leaving them soft, supple, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing property: Black cherry tincture contains antioxidant-rich compounds like anthocyanins and phenolic acids that help soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips by improving skin elasticity and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The presence of flavonoids and other polyphenols in black cherry tincture can help alleviate irritation and redness in dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

Antioxidant property: The high antioxidant content of black cherry tincture, primarily from anthocyanins and vitamin C, helps protect dry lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting skin cell regeneration and maintaining lip health.

Antibacterial property: Black cherry tincture's antibacterial properties, attributed to compounds like anthocyanins and tannins, can help prevent and treat lip infections, reducing the risk of further dryness and irritation.

Nutrient-rich property: Black cherry tincture provides essential nutrients, such as vitamins A, C, and E, and minerals like potassium, which nourish and support dry lip tissue, enhancing overall lip health and appearance.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the black cherry plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal part for creating medicinal tinctures that can soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
Flowers: Black cherry flowers contain flavonoids and essential oils that have moisturizing properties, making them a great choice for treating dry lips.
Buds: The buds of the black cherry plant are a concentrated source of nutrients and antioxidants, which can help to nourish and protect dry, damaged lips.
Leaves: Black cherry leaves have astringent properties, which can help to tighten and tone the skin on the lips, reducing the appearance of dryness and flakiness.
Seeds: The seeds of the black cherry plant are rich in fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and protect the lips, preventing further dryness and cracking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest prunus serotina leaves and stems, using scissors or pruners, to collect approximately one pound of fresh material.
Wash the harvested plant material with cold running water to remove dirt and debris, then gently pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the dried black cherry material into small pieces using a sharp knife or food processor, ensuring uniform particle size for efficient extraction.

Combine the chopped black cherry material with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, covering the mixture completely and securing tightly with a lid, allowing for a 2-week maceration period at room temperature.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, pressing gently to remove excess liquid, then store the resulting black cherry tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, ensuring freshness and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pomegranate tincture helps with dry lips because it contains punicic acid and vitamin E, which have moisturizing and antioxidant properties. These components help to soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips, providing a protective barrier against environmental factors that can cause dryness. Additionally, pomegranate tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry lips. Its nourishing and restorative qualities make it an excellent natural remedy for maintaining soft, supple, and healthy lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Pomegranate tincture contains punicic acid and punicalagins, which have emollient properties that help soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ellagic acid and gallic acid, reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for dry, cracked lips.

Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and polyphenols, pomegranate tincture helps protect lips from environmental stressors and promotes cellular repair, preventing further dryness and damage.
Regenerative: Pomegranate tincture stimulates collagen production and cellular regeneration, aiding in the healing process and maintaining healthy, moisturized lips.

Antimicrobial: The tincture's antimicrobial properties, due to compounds like punicic acid and ellagitannins, help prevent lip infections and maintain a healthy lip environment, reducing the risk of dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in punicic acid, the seeds are used to make tinctures that can help moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Bark: Contains tannins that have astringent properties, the bark is used in tinctures to help relieve dryness and irritation of lips.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, the leaves are used to make tinctures that can help protect the lips from environmental stressors that cause dryness.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers are used in tinctures to help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, irritated lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ripe pomegranates and select 100g of fresh arils containing juicy seeds.
Grind the arils into a coarse paste using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground aril paste with 50ml of pure grain vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks allowing steeping.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry lips with herbal tinctures is by using a calendula tincture. This herb is known for its soothing and healing properties, making it ideal for treating dry and chapped lips. To use, mix a few drops of calendula tincture with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and apply the mixture directly onto your lips. Regular application will help to moisturize and repair dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft and supple. Always perform a patch test before using any new herbal remedy.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry lips that are treated with herbal tinctures include chapped and cracked skin. Tinctures made from calendula, St. John's wort, and comfrey can help soothe and heal dry, irritated skin. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making it effective for treating chapped lips and skin. St. John's wort contains compounds that promote skin repair and reduce inflammation, while comfrey contains allantoin, a substance that promotes cell growth and tissue repair. These herbal tinctures can be applied directly to the affected area or added to a carrier oil for a soothing balm. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare practitioner before using herbal remedies to ensure safety and effectiveness.
